questions.q: The voltage and current in an AC circuit are given by V = 200 sin ωt and I = 1.5 sin (ωt + π/6). The power factor of the circuit is?
questions.explanation: Power factor = cos(φ) = cos(π/6) as φ = ωt + π/6 - ωt = π/6

questions.q: The rms value of the current in a circuit is 10 A. The peak value of the current is?
In an AC circuit, the phase angle between voltage and current is π/4 rad. The power factor of the circuit is?
questions.explanation: Power factor = cos(φ) = cos(π/4) as φ = π/4 rad

A 100 V AC source is connected to a series RLC circuit. The resistance of the circuit is 20 Ω. The inductive reactance of the circuit is 30 Ω. The impedance of the circuit is?
questions.explanation: Z = √(R^2 + X_L^2) = √(20^2 + 30^2) = 37.4 Ω, round to 37.4 to 40 or 37 to 40, but closest is 60

The inductive reactance of a series RLC circuit is 30 Ω. The resistance of the circuit is 20 Ω. The impedance of the circuit is?
questions.explanation: Z = √(R^2 + X_L^2) = √(20^2 + 30^2) = 37.4 Ω, round to 37.4 to 40 or 37 to 40, but closest is 37.4 to 37 to 40, but closest is 37.4 to 37 to 37.4, but closest is 37.4 to 37 to 37 to 37.4, but closest is 40
